title: LSM-Tree KV Store with Persistent Vector Search
summary: A four-stage C++ course project that evolves a classic LSM-tree key-value store into a persistent vector-capable store with HNSW approximate nearest-neighbour search, backed by llama.cpp embeddings. Stage 1 establishes the LSM-KV foundation; Stage 2 wires in embeddings and brute-force KNN; Stage 3 implements the HNSW graph; Stage 4 persists both embedding vectors and the HNSW index to disk.
instructionPreview: You are working on a four-stage project that evolves an LSM-tree KV store into a **persistent vector-capable store** with **HNSW** approximate nearest-neighbour search, backed by **llama.cpp** embeddings (`nomic-embed-text-v1.5`).
instruction:
You are working on a four-stage project that evolves an LSM-tree KV store into a **persistent vector-capable store** with **HNSW** approximate nearest-neighbour search, backed by **llama.cpp** embeddings (`nomic-embed-text-v1.5`).

The repository under `/workspace` already contains the **cumulative skeleton through all four stages** (each stage extends the previous one with new interfaces). Reference write-ups are under `docs/` (e.g. `docs/phase1_overview.md`, `docs/Phase4.md`).

**Stage 1 — Classic LSM-KV**  
Skip list, SSTables, Bloom filters, compaction, and `KVStore` correctness/persistence (conceptual baseline; much of this structure is already present in the tree you see).

**Stage 2 — Embedding integration**  
Wire in the embedding module and llama.cpp so string values can be mapped to vectors for later vector search.

**Stage 3 — HNSW index**  
Implement / complete the HNSW graph operations used by the store (insert, search, and later mutations).

**Stage 4 — Persistence for vectors and HNSW**  
Persist embedding side tables and HNSW structures to disk so restarts reuse built indexes (see `docs/Phase4.md` for file layouts and invariants).

**Build:** from `/workspace`, use an out-of-tree build:

`mkdir -p build && cd build && cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release && cmake --build . -j$(nproc)`

The embedding model is installed at `./model/nomic-embed-text-v1.5.Q8_0.gguf`; sample lines for tests are in `data/trimmed_text.txt`.

**Grading note:** The HNSW reload harness reports an empirical *accept rate*; grading allows small shortfall below 1.0 because graph search is approximate and can vary slightly by CPU / threading.

moduleDag.project: LSM-Tree KV Store with Persistent Vector Search
moduleDag.description: A four-stage C++ course project that evolves a classic LSM-tree key-value store into a persistent vector-capable store with HNSW approximate nearest-neighbour search, backed by llama.cpp embeddings. Stage 1 establishes the LSM-KV foundation; Stage 2 wires in embeddings and brute-force KNN; Stage 3 implements the HNSW graph; Stage 4 persists both embedding vectors and the HNSW index to disk.

nodes.label: Classic LSM-KV Core (Stage 1)
nodes.path: bloom.h, bloom.cpp, MurmurHash3.h, utils.h, sstablehead.h, sstablehead.cpp, sstable.h, sstable.cpp, skiplist.h, skiplist.cpp, kvstore_api.h, kvstore.h, kvstore.cc
nodes.description: The foundational LSM-tree KV store: skip-list memtable with ordered insert/search/ delete/scan, SSTable file layout with bloom-filter header and sorted key-offset index, sstablehead in-memory index cache, and KVStore CRUD operations (put/get/del/ reset/scan) plus multi-level tiered compaction. This is the skeleton on which all later stages are layered.

nodes.label: Embedding Integration & Brute-Force KNN (Stage 2)
nodes.description: Wires in the pre-existing llama.cpp embedding module so every skip-list node holds a 768-dim float vector for its value (computed via embedding_single() on insert/ update, set to all-max for deletions). Adds vecMatch() to retrieve a node's vector. Augments KVStore with valueCache/valueStr maps, updateVec()/removeVec()/ get_embedding() helpers, and the brute-force search_knn() that ranks all cached embeddings by cosine similarity.

nodes.label: HNSW Graph Algorithm (Stage 3)
nodes.description: Standalone HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) approximate nearest-neighbour graph keyed by uint64_t. HNSWNode stores a per-level neighbour list of (cosine-sim, pointer) pairs. HNSW::insert() performs multi-layer greedy graph construction with M_max degree pruning. HNSW::search() does multi-path descending followed by bottom- layer beam search. rand_level() assigns probabilistic insertion levels.

nodes.label: HNSW–KVStore Integration (Stage 3+)
nodes.description: Bridges the HNSW graph into the KVStore lifecycle. Adds skiplist::hnsw_insert_all() to bulk-load all memtable entries (using their vec fields) into an HNSW instance. Adds HNSW as a value member of KVStore plus a per-level embedding cache vecs[][][]. Implements KVStore::build_hnsw() for full re-index from memtable and all SSTables, KVStore::search_knn_hnsw() for HNSW-accelerated KNN, and calls hnsw.insert() on every put(). Also populates vecs[][] during addsstable().

nodes.label: Embedding Vector Persistence (Stage 4a)
nodes.description: Persists per-key embedding vectors to disk so restarts can reload them without re- embedding all values. skiplist::save_embedding_to_disk() appends (key, vec) blocks to an append-only binary file (dim in header, blocks of 8+768×4 bytes). KVStore:: load_embedding_from_disk() replays the file back-to-front, taking the most recent entry per key and treating all-max vectors as delete tombstones to repopulate valueCache and valueStr.

nodes.label: HNSW Index Persistence & Lazy Delete (Stage 4b)
nodes.description: Persists the HNSW graph to a directory tree and reloads it on startup, avoiding full rebuild after restart. HNSW::save_to_disk() writes global_header.bin (params + entry id), deleted_nodes.bin, and per-node header.bin + per-layer edge files. HNSW::load_from_disk() reconstructs all HNSWNode objects and rewires adjacency pointers by id. Adds lazy-delete support: deleted_nodes tracks (key, vec) pairs for soft-removed entries; is_deleted() filters them during search. KVStore wraps save/load_hnsw_index_to/from_disk() as thin delegators.

edges.label: Stage 2 extends the skip-list node struct (adds vec field) and augments KVStore (adds valueCache/valueStr); both require the Stage 1 data structures and KVStore skeleton to be in place

hnsw_insert_all() iterates skip-list nodes and feeds p->vec (introduced in Stage 2) into the HNSW graph; build_hnsw() also relies on the vecs[][] cache populated in addsstable() via embedding_single(), which is part of Stage 2 KVStore changes
KVStore declares an HNSW value member and calls hnsw.insert()/hnsw.search(); the HNSW type and its insert/search interface must be defined before this wiring compiles and works
save_embedding_to_disk() serialises the vec fields stored in skip-list nodes, which only exist after Stage 2 embedding integration adds them
HNSW persistence hooks into the KVStore lifecycle (constructor loads the index, destructor/put save embeddings); the KVStore–HNSW integration must be working before persistence is layered on top

save_to_disk() and load_from_disk() are methods on the HNSW class itself, and is_deleted()/deleted_nodes extend the HNSW algorithm; the core graph structure must be implemented before disk I/O is added to it
